title: Generate ephemeral session seed
overview: Implement a deterministic session seed generator that combines browser signals, geo hint, and current timestamp into a stable hash. The seed is computed once at session start and remains constant for the session lifetime. No source data is stored — only the derived seed persists in memory.

steps.title: Write tests for sessionSeed module
details: Create describe block: 'US-007: Generate ephemeral session seed'
Test: seed includes a time component — different timestamps produce different seeds
Test: seed changes on reload — different signal snapshots yield different seeds
Test: seed remains stable during one session — calling the getter multiple times returns the same value
Test: seed is derived without storing source data — only the hash string is retained, not the original inputs
Test: deterministic — identical inputs always produce the same hash
Test: handles null geo values gracefully (country/region null)
Test: handles null/missing optional signal values (devicePixelRatio null, hardwareConcurrency null)
Test: no forbidden storage APIs accessed (localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies)
Test: seed is a non-empty string of consistent format
conventions: Follow existing test pattern: vi.spyOn for browser APIs, vi.fn for mocks
Mock readSignals() and fetchGeoHint() to control inputs
Use beforeEach to reset module state between tests

steps.title: Implement sessionSeed module
details: Define SeedInputs interface: { signals: BrowserSignals; geo: GeoHint; timestamp: number }
Implement a simple synchronous deterministic hash function (djb2 or FNV-1a) — no crypto dependency needed since this is not security-sensitive
Implement hashInputs(inputs: SeedInputs): string — serializes inputs to a canonical string, then hashes to a hex/base36 string
Implement createSessionSeed(signals: BrowserSignals, geo: GeoHint): string — captures Date.now() at call time, combines with signals and geo, returns hash
Export a module-level singleton pattern: initSessionSeed(signals, geo) sets the seed once, getSessionSeed() returns it thereafter
getSessionSeed() throws if called before init to catch usage errors
No source data retained after hashing — only the derived string is kept in the module closure
steps.hash_approach: Use a simple string hash (djb2 or FNV-1a) operating on JSON.stringify of a sorted/canonical input object. Output as base-36 string for compactness. No need for crypto.subtle since this is a scene seed, not a security primitive.

steps.title: Wire sessionSeed into main.ts
details: Import readSignals from src/input/signals
Import initSessionSeed from src/seed/sessionSeed
After geoPromise resolves, call readSignals() and initSessionSeed(signals, geo)
Log the seed with console.debug('[EAVI] session seed:', seed)
Seed is now available via getSessionSeed() for downstream visual modules

steps.title: Run tests and build
details: Run vitest to confirm all new and existing tests pass
Run npm run build to confirm production build succeeds
Verify no console errors, no forbidden API usage

acceptance_verification.seed_includes_time_component: hashInputs uses timestamp field; test confirms different timestamps yield different seeds
acceptance_verification.seed_changes_on_reload: Each call to initSessionSeed captures fresh Date.now(); test with different mock times confirms different output
acceptance_verification.seed_remains_stable_during_session: Module singleton pattern — initSessionSeed sets once, getSessionSeed always returns same value; test calls getter multiple times
acceptance_verification.seed_derived_without_storing_source_data: After hashing, only the string result is stored in the module closure; original signals/geo/timestamp are not retained; test verifies module exports no references to source data

technical_notes.hash_function: djb2 or FNV-1a — both are fast, deterministic, and produce good distribution for non-cryptographic use. No external dependency required.
technical_notes.serialization: JSON.stringify with sorted keys to ensure deterministic input ordering across environments
technical_notes.no_pointer_entropy: Pointer entropy is intentionally excluded from the session seed — it is a per-frame signal consumed by the render loop, not a session-level input. The seed must be stable for the session.
technical_notes.timestamp_granularity: Date.now() milliseconds — sufficient for uniqueness across reloads
